---
title: "OrgPath Narrative"
subtitle: "A fifteen-document reading sequence on the governance substrate Microsoft did not build"
doc-type: section-landing
audience: [Executive, Technical, Customer]
classification: Controlled
boundary: GCC-Moderate
family: orgpath-narrative
canon-source: "src/uiao/canon/UIAO_007_OrgTree_Modernization_AD_to_EntraID_v1.0.md"
listing:
  - id: chapters
    contents:
      - "0*-*.qmd"
      - "1*-*.qmd"
    type: table
    fields: [title, subtitle]
    sort: "series-order asc"
    sort-ui: false
    filter-ui: false
---

# OrgPath Narrative {.unnumbered}

A fifteen-document reading sequence written in continuous narrative voice —
no bullets, no itemization, no machine-style decomposition. The series
examines the structural gap at the center of the Microsoft cloud identity
stack and the OrgTree / OrgPath governance substrate that closes it.

The early documents do not lead with UIAO. They begin with an architect's
honest accounting of what Microsoft's native identity and governance
products actually provide, then articulate what a governance substrate
must contain to be useful, and finally introduce OrgTree and OrgPath as
the proposed answer. The middle of the sequence walks the OrgPath
attribute through every Microsoft surface that consumes it — Intune,
Defender, Purview, Application Identity, Azure Policy, Cross-Tenant B2B,
Sentinel / PIM / RBAC, DNS / Cloud PKI, Power BI — and the supplement at
the end addresses third-party DDI integration with Infoblox and BlueCat.
The final document is the synthesis: the UIAO Governance OS narrative
that ties the series together.

The documents are published here unchanged from their authored form.
Each carries Quarto front matter for site rendering and DOCX export, but
the prose body is the original manuscript without revision. Where a
document references a UIAO architectural decision or canon artifact,
readers should consult the linked canon for the normative version; this
series is the explanatory companion.

## Reading sequence {#chapters}

::: {#chapters}
:::

## Companion canon

For the normative substrate specifications and architectural decisions
this narrative paraphrases:

- [Modernization → OrgTree](../../../modernization/orgtree.qmd) — the
  canonical OrgTree / OrgPath specification (`UIAO_007`).
- [Architecture Decision Records](../../adr/index.qmd) — the binding
  record of UIAO architectural decisions. The OrgPath substrate is
  governed primarily by `ADR-035` (codebook binding), `ADR-036` (dynamic
  group provisioning), `ADR-037` (administrative unit provisioning),
  `ADR-038` (device-plane OrgPath), `ADR-039` (policy targeting),
  `ADR-040` (drift engine), `ADR-041` (UIAO Git infrastructure), and
  `ADR-048` (OrgPath attribute selection).

## Audience

Federal IT architects, governance engineers, authorizing officials, and
program leadership engaged in Active Directory to Entra ID modernization.
The reading sequence assumes operating familiarity with Active Directory,
Entra ID, Microsoft Intune, Microsoft Defender, Microsoft Purview, Azure
Policy, and Conditional Access. Readers without that background will get
more from the
[Executive Governance Series](../executive-governance-series/index.qmd)
or the
[Whitepapers](../whitepapers/index.qmd) section first.

## Download

Every chapter renders to Microsoft Word (`.docx`) alongside the web view
(see the **DOCX** button in the page header on each chapter).
